_comment: Sample ADO REST API responses for the shared-ci-platform project. These represent what you would get by querying Azure DevOps APIs directly. This data supplements the YAML files in the repo with pipeline-level metadata that only ADO knows: pipeline IDs, enabled/disabled state, run history, variable groups, service connections, environments, and agent pools.

build_definitions._description: All pipeline definitions in the project. Each entry is a pipeline registered in ADO, including its YAML path, repo reference, queue/pool, triggers, variable groups, and enabled state.

_meta.notes: Original team disbanded. shared-ci-platform maintains as best-effort.
Still references master branch for templates. Should migrate to main.
Manual trigger only. Used to test template changes across branches before committing. References 4 different template branches via parameter.

Uses staging/preprod template branch for retry logic. Has a service-local template fork at pipeline-fragments/build-python-local.yml that is not currently referenced by this pipeline but exists as a fallback.
Legacy pipeline, trigger disabled in YAML. Still triggered externally by downstream jobs. Uses legacy/master-support frozen branch. Last manually triggered Jan 2026.
Uses alt-templates/frontend/ from team/frontend-custom branch. team-frontend owns both the service and the templates.

Hybrid: uses team-overrides template from team/reporting-hotfix branch + inline compliance steps. Looks like a build but is really a compliance workflow. Runs on schedule AND CI trigger. 365-day retention for audit purposes.
Fully inline Rust pipeline. No shared templates — team-quant built independently. Deploys outside release-orchestrator to a compute cluster.
Custom inline Go pipeline. Central templates now have build-go.yml but this service predates it and was never migrated.

NON-BUILD. Executes Jupyter notebooks on self-hosted linux-build-workers as free compute. 3-hour timeout. Runs nightly. No owner identified. Requires internal network access. Cannot move to GHA hosted runners.

NON-BUILD. Monte Carlo simulations on high-memory-pool self-hosted agents. 6-hour timeout. Also manually triggered with custom parameters. No owner. Cannot move to GHA hosted runners — needs high-memory compute + internal network.
NON-BUILD. Nightly VaR scenario sweep (50,000 scenarios). Runs weeknights on high-memory-pool. Uploads results to data-warehouse. 4-hour timeout. Needs internal network + high-memory compute.

NON-BUILD. Daily data export to downstream reporting. Runs weekdays at 7 PM ET (23:00 UTC). Uses linux-build-workers for internal DB access via pyodbc. Distributes Excel reports to team-reporting and team-quant.

NON-BUILD. Weekly compliance attestation backfill. Scans compliance-store for gaps, regenerates missing records. Uses linux-build-workers for compliance-store network access. 365-day retention for audit.
Ad-hoc manual pipeline. Used for bulk trade reprocessing after data corrections. Last run Feb 2026. 8-hour timeout. Unclear ownership.
Paused in ADO. One-time migration pipeline created Aug 2024 — should have been deleted. Last run Aug 2024. Candidate for deletion.
Disabled in ADO. Replaced by pricing-engine-ci (ID 101). Last run June 2023. Kept for reference. Uses legacy/master-support frozen branch + inline .NET 6 build.
Disabled in ADO. Original batch runner that mixed build + compute in one pipeline. Replaced by risk-batch-ci + night-jobs. Last run (failed) Feb 2023. Python 3.8.

build_runs_summary._description: Summarized run history per pipeline over the last 90 days. Shows frequency, success rate, and average duration — data only available from ADO, not from YAML.

variable_groups._description: Shared variable groups referenced by pipelines. These are configured at the ADO project level, NOT in YAML. Any migration must map these to GitHub Actions secrets/variables or environment-level config.

value.name: shared-ci-secrets
value.description: Common secrets shared across all CI pipelines

value.name: internal-network-credentials
value.description: Service account credentials for accessing internal systems from build agents

value.name: data-warehouse-credentials
value.description: Credentials for uploading results to the data warehouse

value.name: positions-db-credentials
value.description: Database credentials for positions data (used by exports and migration)

service_connections._description: Service connections configured at the project level. These provide authenticated access to external services during pipeline runs. Must be mapped to GitHub Actions OIDC, secrets, or service account tokens.

value.name: AzureSubscription-Dev
value.description: Azure Resource Manager connection for dev environment deployments

value.name: AzureSubscription-Staging
value.description: Azure Resource Manager connection for staging environment

value.name: AzureSubscription-Prod
value.description: Azure Resource Manager connection for production deployments

value.name: ContainerRegistry-ACR
value.description: Azure Container Registry for Docker image publishing

value.name: GitHub-SourceMirror
value.description: GitHub connection used for syncing upstream references

environments._description: Deployment environments with approval/check configurations. These are configured in ADO's UI and gate deployments — they must be recreated as GitHub Environments with equivalent protection rules.

settings.instructions: Verify staging test results before proceeding
Review compliance attestation and artifact baseline comparison
Production deployment requires release manager AND service owner approval. Verify compliance attestation is present.

settings.description: Only one production deployment at a time

settings.instructions: Verify SSR bundle and CDN purge before promoting

agent_pools._description: Agent pools available in the organization. Hosted pools map cleanly to GitHub-hosted runners. Self-hosted pools require self-hosted GHA runners or alternative infrastructure. This is critical for non-build workloads.

value.name: Azure Pipelines
value._migration_note: Maps directly to GitHub-hosted runners (ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest). No migration concern.

value.name: linux-build-workers
value._migration_note: Self-hosted pool with internal network access. Pipelines using this pool need internal network connectivity — cannot migrate to GitHub-hosted runners without network peering or a self-hosted GHA runner deployed in the same network segment.

value.name: high-memory-pool
value._migration_note: High-memory self-hosted pool (128GB RAM, 32 cores). Used exclusively by non-build compute workloads (Monte Carlo, VaR). These workloads should NOT migrate to GHA — they need dedicated compute infrastructure (K8s jobs, AWS Batch, Azure Container Instances, etc.).

value.name: windows-build-workers
value._migration_note: Legacy Windows self-hosted pool. Not currently referenced by any active pipeline YAML but may be used by pipelines with ADO-level pool overrides. Half offline. Candidate for decommission after migration audit.
